cli.welcome: Welcome to VulnClaw! Enter a natural-language task to start. Type help for commands. Press Ctrl+C to exit.
cli.no_api_key: [!] No LLM API key detected. Run [bold]vulnclaw config set llm.api_key <your-key>[/] first.
cli.offline_mode: [dim]Starting in offline mode: local tools only, no AI reasoning.[/]
cli.press_again: [dim]Press Ctrl+C again within 3 seconds to exit REPL[/]
cli.no_target_for_report: [!] Set a target first with [bold]target <host>[/] or run [bold]report <host>[/].
report.rec.review_surface: Prioritize reviewing the attack surface and reinforcing the verification chain; confirm high-risk entry points are remediated.
report.rec.none_high: No high-risk findings yet; continue deeper testing.
cli.enter_auto_mode: [dim][*] Entering autonomous pentest mode. Press Ctrl+C to interrupt.[/]
cli.auto_mode_hint: [dim][*] Auto mode stays active. Type [bold]chat[/] to switch to single-turn mode.[/]
cli.auto_resume_hint: [dim]Press [bold]Enter[/] to resume auto pentest, or type a new command.[/]
cli.init.step_provider: 1. Choose a provider: [bold]vulnclaw config provider minimax[/]
cli.init.step_api_key: 2. Set API key: [bold]vulnclaw config set llm.api_key <your-key>[/]
help.title: VulnClaw Help
help.report: [cyan]report <host>[/]      Generate a report from the current/selected target
help.think_on_off: [cyan]think on/off[/]       Explicitly show or hide thinking
help.persistent: [cyan]persistent[/]         Start persistent testing for the current target
help.persistent_host: [cyan]persistent <host>[/]  Start persistent testing for a specific target
help.chat: [cyan]chat / manual[/]       Exit auto mode, switch to single-turn chat
help.auto_mode_desc: [dim]Enter a natural-language task that includes a target and VulnClaw will run autonomously.[/]
help.auto_mode_stays: [dim]Auto mode persists after completion. Type [bold]chat[/] to exit.[/]
help.persistent_mode_desc: [dim]Runs multi-round autonomous testing, generates reports, and continues across cycles.[/]
status.title: Current Status
auto_pentest.title: Autonomous Pentest Result
persistent.title: Persistent Pentest
tui.title: VulnClaw TUI Workbench
tui.desc: Terminal tool for authorized security testing: Confirm target and boundaries before starting.
tui.mode_quick_desc: Only recon and basic risk identification. Suitable for first-time assessment.
tui.mode_standard_desc: Recon + vulnerability discovery. Default recommendation. No active high-risk verification.
tui.mode_deep_desc: More thorough vulnerability verification. Requires authorization confirmation.
tui.mode_continuous_desc: Multi-round continuous testing. Suitable for CTF or long-term monitoring.
tui.cli_note: Original CLI/REPL is still the default; TUI only activates with vulnclaw tui.
tui.press_again: [dim]Press Ctrl+C again within 3 seconds to exit TUI[/]
tui.diagnostic_footer: This is an inline TUI diagnostic summary. Run vulnclaw doctor for full details.
tui.enter_target: Enter authorized target URL / domain / IP
tui.select_provider: Select Provider (leave empty to keep current)
tui.api_key: API Key (leave empty to keep current)
tui.enter_allowed_actions: Allowed actions (comma-separated, leave empty for mode defaults)
tui.enter_blocked_actions: Blocked actions (comma-separated, leave empty for mode defaults)
tui.confirm_deep_mode: {mode} may perform deeper or multi-round verification. Confirm target and scope are authorized?
tui.name: Name
tui.enter_api_key: API Key (leave empty to keep current)
tui.task_returned: Task returned, press Enter to go back to TUI
tui.enter_task_mode: [dim][*] Entering security check mode. Press Ctrl+C to interrupt.[/]
tui.no_history_for_target: No history for this target yet.
tui.slash_target: Set authorized target URL / domain / IP
tui.slash_mode: Select check mode (quick / standard / deep / continuous)
tui.slash_config: Configure LLM provider / model / API key
tui.error_invalid_port: Port must be a number between 1-65535
tui.prompt_enter_model_fallback: Failed to fetch models, enter model name manually (current: {model}):
tui.no_models_available: No models available from this provider
skill.exploitation.description: Exploitation workflow — build PoCs to verify and exploit discovered vulnerabilities
skill.pentest-flow.description: End-to-end pentest orchestration — from recon through report generation
skill.hackerone.description: HackerOne bounty scope-guard flow — reads the program scope, enforces scope and program rules, then hands each in-scope asset to pentest-flow
skill.post-exploitation.description: Post-exploitation workflow — internal recon and lateral movement
skill.recon.description: Reconnaissance workflow — passive and active recon
skill.reporting.description: Reporting workflow — generate structured pentest reports and PoCs
skill.vuln-discovery.description: Vulnerability discovery — scan for vulnerabilities based on recon findings
skill.waf-bypass.description: WAF bypass techniques — assorted methods for evading WAFs
skill.ai-mcp-security.description: AI & MCP security assessment — prompt injection, tool abuse, MCP trust boundaries, agent privilege escape, data leakage, model risk, GAARM risk matrix
skill.android-pentest.description: Android app pentest — APK analysis, hooking, automated testing, runtime driving, signature recovery, traffic capture
skill.client-reverse.description: Client reversing & Burp replay — signature recovery, decryption, request-chain tracing, and stable replay for authorized Android app, browser JS, and desktop client testing
skill.crypto-toolkit.description: Encoding & crypto toolkit — base64/URL/hex/HTML-entity codecs, MD5/SHA hashing, AES/DES/RSA, JWT parsing, Caesar/ROT13, rail-fence/Vigenère, Unicode escapes, Morse, and more
skill.ctf-crypto.description: CTF cryptography attacks — RSA (low-exponent/common-modulus/Wiener/Coppersmith), AES (padding oracle/ECB byte-flip/GCM nonce reuse), ECC, LFSR/LCG/PRNG, classical ciphers, LWE lattice attacks
skill.ctf-misc.description: CTF misc knowledge — Python/Bash jail escapes, encoding-chain identification & decoding, QR/audio/image stego, game-VM reversing, CTFd API navigation, Linux privesc
skill.ctf-web.description: CTF web attacks — PHP loose-comparison bypass, command-injection space bypass, eval echo tricks, SSTI chains, deserialization chains, PHP code-audit checklist, common flag locations
skill.cve-triage.description: CVE lookup and triage — map discovered services/versions to known CVEs, score by CVSS/exploitability, and prioritize what to verify first
skill.intranet-pentest-advanced.descript: Advanced intranet pentest — lateral movement, credential theft, privilege escalation, persistence, tunneling/proxying, AD attacks, ADCS abuse, Exchange/SharePoint attacks
skill.osint-recon.description: OSINT reconnaissance — four-dimension collection model (server → website → domain → people); the people dimension triggers conditionally
skill.pentest-tools.description: Pentest tool cheatsheet — codecs, reverse shells, red-team tooling, exploitation, password attacks, intranet tools, credential theft, privesc, tunneling, system commands, recon, domain attacks, web & Windows tools
skill.rapid-checklist.description: Rapid pentest checklist & payloads — payload families, bypass reminders, verification order, and quick test cards for when the direction is already known
skill.secknowledge-skill.description: Web + AI security testing knowledge base — WooYun 88,636 cases + Xianzhi L1–L4 methodology + GAARM 150 risks + OWASP
skill.web-pentest.description: Web application pentest — full workflow covering tech-stack fingerprinting, directory enumeration, auth testing, input validation, and logic flaws
skill.web-security-advanced.description: Advanced web security testing — injection families, protocol security, auth & logic flaws, file & deployment security, and the modern web attack surface, with full playbooks
agent.runtime.failure_history: Failure history (do not repeat these actions):
agent.runtime.unverified_assumptions: ⚠️ Unverified assumptions (used for reasoning but not confirmed and may be wrong):
agent.runtime.assumption_caution: → If an assumption is wrong, all reasoning based on it is invalid. Verify critical assumptions first.
agent.runtime.path_stuck_warning:
⚠️ You have tried the current path for several rounds without a breakthrough.
Re-examine the source and available information for a simpler exploitation path.
List all possible paths, then switch to the simplest one.
agent.runtime.path_switch_warning:
🔴 Forced path-switch directive: you have failed on the same attack path {count} times.
You must immediately:
1. Stop and list at least three completely different alternative attack paths
   (change the attack method, not just the payload value; for example, move from regex bypass to wrapper-based file reads or array bypass)
2. Rank the alternatives from easiest to hardest
3. Start with the simplest alternative
4. Spend one round verifying the new assumption before trying the new path

⚠️ Do not continue changing payload values on the same path.
agent.runtime.assumption_checkpoint:
🧠 Assumption-verification checkpoint:
Before the next step, take ten seconds to ask:
1. What assumptions does my current reasoning rely on?
2. Have I verified them, or am I taking them for granted?
3. Would my reasoning chain collapse if one assumption were wrong?
4. Can I spend one round sending a request that verifies the most critical assumption?

❌ Common fatal assumptions: preg_replace replaces only the first match / a Python simulation matches server behavior / the parameter name has a particular value
agent.runtime.python_timeout_warning:
⚠️ **Code execution warning**: the previous Python script timed out.
Do not write complex scripts longer than 10 lines.
Prefer existing tools (fetch/python_execute) over custom crawlers or parsers.
Do not rerun the same large script.
agent.runtime.blocked_targets_warning:
🚨 **Unreachable target warning**: the following targets have failed repeatedly and must not be retried:
{targets}

You must:
1. Stop accessing those targets immediately
2. Focus on other reachable targets
3. If no other targets remain, deepen exploitation of confirmed vulnerabilities
4. Do not waste more rounds connecting to unreachable targets
agent.runtime.dead_loop_severe:
🔴 Severe warning: {count} consecutive rounds produced no new findings.
This indicates a dead loop. Immediately take one of these actions:
1. 🔥 Fetch the complete source again (using python_execute + strip_tags)
2. 🔥 Try a completely different attack path (change parameter, method, or tool)
3. 🔥 If information is insufficient, acknowledge it and try another recon method
4. 🔥 Stop repeating the same action. Review the failure history and choose a new direction

⚠️ Repeating the same action will not produce a different result.
agent.runtime.dead_loop_warning:
⚠️ Warning: {count} consecutive rounds produced no new findings.
Check whether you are repeating an action or overlooking an untried path.
If the current method is not working, switch methods immediately.
agent.runtime.flag_unverified:
⚠️ You previously claimed this flag: {flag}
The flag has not been independently verified. You must:
1. Resend the payload with a tool and confirm the result is reproducible
2. Or cross-check it with another method (for example, use another function or path to read the same content)
3. If verification fails, acknowledge that the earlier flag was wrong and continue solving
Do not mark [DONE] until verification is complete.
agent.runtime.ctf_no_flag:
🔴 CTF mode — find and verify the flag.
No flag has been found, so do not mark [DONE].
Analyze the available information and continue with the most likely attack path.
If the current path is blocked, switch to another path.
agent.runtime.ctf_unverified_flag:
🔴 CTF mode — the claimed flag is not verified.
Verify the flag with a tool before marking [DONE].
If verification fails, continue searching for the correct flag.
agent.runtime.recon_incomplete:
🔴 Reconnaissance is incomplete. Unchecked dimensions remain, so do not mark [DONE].
Continue checking incomplete dimensions until each has been covered for at least one round.
agent.runtime.recon_trigger_complete: All dimensions are complete ✅ and {count} consecutive rounds produced no progress
agent.runtime.recon_trigger_safety: {count} consecutive rounds produced no progress (8+5 safety valve)
agent.runtime.recon_force_switch:
🔴 ★★★ Forced reconnaissance → exploitation transition ★★★
{reason}.
Switch to the exploitation stage immediately instead of collecting more information or saving a report.

★ Do this now:
1. Output `phase: vuln_discovery` in the response
2. Use the collected recon (target profile, related hosts, leaked APIs, and other results) to exploit the highest-value attack surface
3. Do not save another recon report or call recon tools
4. Do not repeat existing findings; perform a new verification step

★ Output directory (the framework saves recon reports automatically):
   {output_dir}
⚠️ The objective is successful exploitation, not a recon report.
agent.runtime.recon_min_rounds: 🔴 Minimum reconnaissance rounds: this is round {round}; at least 8 rounds are required. Continue deeper even if coverage seems sufficient.
agent.runtime.next_action_instruction:
Decide the next action based on the current state and all previous findings, and keep advancing the penetration test.
Do not repeat completed actions; focus on meaningful forward progress.
If you find an important lead or complete the test, add [DONE] at the end of the response.
agent.summary.prompt:
Target: {target}  |  Current phase: {phase}

=== Executed Steps ===
{steps}

=== Key Observations / Results ===
{notes}

=== Vulnerability Findings ===
{findings}

Write a detailed attack-path narrative in English that includes:
1. The exact URLs and paths tested (for example, https://target.com/admin/login)
2. The specific technique or tool used at each step (for example, SQLMap blind injection, directory enumeration, or nmap port scanning)
3. Important response characteristics (for example, a 155-byte length difference or HTTP 500 error disclosure)
4. How vulnerabilities relate to the attack surface (for example, directory enumeration found /manager/html, which matched CVE-2023-44487)
5. Any subdomains found (for example, api.target.com or cms.target.com)
Format: natural paragraphs, no list, 200–400 words, English only, and no <thinking> tags.
agent.reflexion.hint.raw_payload: First try the raw payload without encoding.
agent.reflexion.hint.html_entity: Use HTML entity encoding for browser-facing injection points.
agent.reflexion.hint.alternative_functio: Use an equivalent alternative function to bypass a blocked function.
agent.reflexion.hint.multilayer_encoding: Combine multiple layers of encoding and obfuscation.
agent.reflexion.hint.alternative_syntax: Use alternative syntax for the same goal (for example, HANDLER instead of SELECT).
agent.reflexion.hint.blind_or_oob: Confirm with time-based blind injection or an out-of-band (OOB) channel.
agent.reflexion.hint.switch_attack_surfa: Switch to a completely different vulnerability type or attack surface.
agent.reflexion.takeover_title: 🔴 Reflexion takeover (repeated failures require a strategy change):
agent.reflexion.stop_payload_retries: - Stop changing payloads on the current attack path.
agent.reflexion.review_assumptions: - Review the failure history and identify which earlier assumption is likely wrong.
agent.reflexion.choose_different_path: - Before trying another payload, choose a materially different attack path or vulnerability type.
agent.reflexion.forced_escalation: - ⚠️ Forced escalation: switch to a completely different vulnerability type or attack surface and stop pursuing the current direction.
agent.reflexion.suggest.env_constraint: Use encoding or obfuscation to bypass filters, switch protocols or endpoints, and confirm access restrictions (WAF, permissions, or rate limits).
agent.reflexion.suggest.path_error: Lower this path's priority and switch to a different attack surface or vulnerability type.
agent.reflexion.suggest.param_error: Adjust parameter names, delimiters, payload syntax, or the injection location.
agent.reflexion.suggest.info_needed: Gather more reconnaissance before retrying this path.
agent.reflexion.suggest.unknown: Review the failure history and try a different approach.
agent.blackboard.executed_tools: executed_tools (do not repeat an already executed tool + arguments):
agent.finding.auto_description_fallback: Automatically detected via a natural-language vulnerability pattern.
agent.loop.persistent_cycle: [Persistent Cycle {cycle}] Continue penetration testing target {target}. This is cycle {cycle}; retain all previous findings and investigate further.
agent.recon.space_missing_query: [!] space_search requires a query or domain argument
agent.recon.passive_skipped: No internet asset search key configured; skipped passive aggregation
agent.recon.verdict.unauth_confirmed: 🔴 Unauthorized access confirmed (no-token response matches token response)
agent.recon.unauth_missing_base: [!] unauth_test requires base_url (or a full URL in endpoints)
agent.recon.unauth_missing_endpoints: [!] unauth_test requires endpoints (API paths/URLs, usually from js_recon)
agent.recon.unauth_heading: # Unauthorized Access Probe — {host}  probed {count} endpoints, {leads} possible leads
agent.recon.manual_review_heading: ⚠ Priority manual review (confirm access to other users' or sensitive data):
agent.recon.dir_global_200: [!] dir_enum stopped: random path {path} returned 200 (length {length}); the target appears to return 200 for arbitrary paths, so directory enumeration is not meaningful.
agent.recon.status_incomplete_instructio: → {count} dimensions remain unchecked. Continue collection; do not mark [DONE]
mcp.router.reason.http_probe_target: HTTP request to probe the target
mcp.router.reason.browser_visit_target: Visit the target in the browser
mcp.router.reason.screenshot_target: Screenshot to record the target page
mcp.router.reason.proxy_detect_request: Craft a detection request via the proxy
input_analysis.hint_target_vuln: The user explicitly told you that [{target}] has a [{vuln_str}] vulnerability.
input_analysis.hint_directive_1: -> You must immediately craft and send a PoC test request!
input_analysis.hint_directive_2: -> Use the fetch tool to send the request directly and observe the real response!
input_analysis.hint_directive_3: -> Do not explore paths or do recon first — test the vulnerability directly!
input_analysis.hint_vuln_only: The user asked you to test for [{vuln_str}].
input_analysis.hint_directive_no_target: -> Immediately craft a PoC test based on the target information already discovered; do not do additional recon first!
input_analysis.payload_sql_boolean_2: GET {target}?id=1' AND 1=2--  -> is the length different?
input_analysis.payload_xss_1: GET {target}?q=<script>alert(1)</script>  -> does the page reflect this content?
input_analysis.payload_rce_1: GET {target}?cmd=whoami  -> observe whether there is command output
